zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard James Stephens於1551年5月18日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾易亞斯廷頓時，他的父親Richard Stevens11歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Andrews37歲。他於1570年與Margaret St. Loe在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾易亞斯廷頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1594年10月19日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾易亞斯廷頓享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾易亞斯廷頓。

nameMeanings.name: Stephens
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Stevens
meanings: English (London), Flemish, Dutch, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Steven . The surname of Flemish origin is also found in the Walloon part of Belgium. In North America, the English form of the surname has also absorbed some like-sounding Jewish names and various other European cognates, e.g. Greek Stefanidis , Serbian Stevanović (see Stevanovic ), Slovenian and Slovak Štefanič (see Stefanic ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Richard James Stephens was born on 18 May 1551, in Eastington, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Richard Stevens, was 11 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Andrews, was 37. He married Margaret St. Loe in 1570, in Eastington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 19 October 1594, in Eastington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Eastington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Margaret St. Loe en 1570, à Eastington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 19 octobre 1594, à Eastington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Eastington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard James Stephensが1551年5月18日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアエアスティントンで生まれた時，父Richard Stevensは11歳，母Lady Elizabeth Andrewsは37歳であった。1570年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアエアスティントンでMargaret St. Loeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1594年10月19日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアエアスティントンで43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアエアスティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard James Stephens(이)가 1551년 5월 18일 영국 그루스스터실르 이스틴그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Stevens(은)는 11세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Andrews(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 이스틴그턴에서 Margaret St. Loe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1594년 10월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 이스틴그턴에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 이스틴그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard James Stephens nasceu em 18 de maio de 1551, em Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Stevens, tinha 11 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Andrews, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret St. Loe em 1570, em Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de outubro de 1594, em Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard James Stephens родился 18 мая 1551 г. в местности эастингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Stevens было 11 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Andrews, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret St. Loe в 1570 г. в местности эастингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 19 октября 1594 г. в местности эастингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности эастингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard James Stephens nació el 18 de mayo de 1551, en Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Stevens, tenía 11 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Andrews, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Margaret St. Loe en 1570, en Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 19 de octubre de 1594, en Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Eastington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
